name: Milda
description: Milda is a significant goddess in Baltic mythology, particularly revered in Lithuanian traditions. She embodies love, freedom, and beauty, representing a powerful feminine force that governs relationships and personal autonomy. Her origins are somewhat shrouded in mystery, with some scholars suggesting she may be a later development in the Baltic pantheon, possibly influenced by other European deities. Milda is often depicted as a beautiful woman, sometimes associated with the moon and with springtime. Her influence extends to romantic love, familial bonds, and the liberation of the spirit. She is seen as a protector of lovers and those seeking personal independence. Offerings were made to her, often involving flowers and other symbols of beauty and affection. While specific myths directly attributed to Milda are relatively scarce compared to other deities like Perkūnas or Saule, her presence is felt throughout Baltic folklore and in the enduring traditions celebrating love and freedom. She represents the pursuit of happiness and the importance of personal fulfillment within the framework of relationships. Milda's enduring appeal lies in her embodiment of these universal values, making her a cherished figure in Baltic cultural heritage. Her connection to the moon links her to cycles and rhythms, emphasizing the natural ebbs and flows of love and life. She is a reminder that true love is not about constraint but about mutual respect and the freedom to be oneself.
subtitle: Goddess of Love, Freedom, and Beauty

keyMyths.title: Milda and the Linden Tree
keyMyths.description: Milda's sacred tree is the linden, symbolizing love and protection. It is said that those who seek her favor should leave offerings beneath its branches.

keyMyths.title: Milda's Gift of Freedom
keyMyths.description: Milda grants individuals the freedom to choose their own paths in love and life. She encourages self-discovery and independence within relationships.

keyMyths.title: Milda's Lunar Connection
keyMyths.description: The moon is associated with Milda, reflecting her influence over the cycles of life and the ebb and flow of emotions. She is seen as a guiding light in matters of the heart.

sources.author: Marija Gimbutas
sources.work: The Goddesses and Gods of Old Europe
sources.description: Provides context for understanding Baltic goddesses within a broader European framework.
sources.text: Provides context for understanding Baltic goddesses within a broader European framework.
